title: Kaworu and the coffins
wikitext:
[]: Kaworu and the coffins             Quote Post by T. K. Simon » Sat Aug 14, 2021 5:53 pm So...... every time kaworu comes out of a coffin, does that mean how many times it has been in a loop? How does the book of life work and what effect does it have on it?

[]: Re: Kaworu and the coffins             Quote Post by rangure0412 » Sat Aug 14, 2021 5:58 pm There's at least 40 opened coffins. So there's at least 37 Evangelion realities that we don't see. The book of the life is something very very abstract its never mentioned before and how it works is a completely mistery

[]: Re: Kaworu and the coffins             Quote Post by T. K. Simon » Sat Aug 14, 2021 6:14 pm rangure0412 wrote: There's at least 40 opened coffins. So there's at least 37 Evangelion realities that we don't see. The book of the life is something very very abstract its never mentioned before and how it works is a completely mistery

[]: Re: Kaworu and the coffins             Quote Post by rangure0412 » Sat Aug 14, 2021 6:23 pm T. K. Simon wrote: rangure0412#928459 wrote: There's at least 40 opened coffins. So there's at least 37 Evangelion realities that we don't see. The book of the life is something very very abstract its never mentioned before and how it works is a completely mistery

[]: Re: Kaworu and the coffins             Quote Post by Konja7 » Sat Aug 14, 2021 6:34 pm rangure0412 wrote: There's at least 40 opened coffins. So there's at least 37 Evangelion realities that we don't see.

[]: Re: Kaworu and the coffins             Quote Post by gchristnsn » Sun Aug 15, 2021 6:51 am This may be a move a la Kurt Vonnegut. Kaworu may exist above the human Universe, and therefore above time, so death and life are the same for him, as he asserts at the original series. The coffins may just be a fancy way of Seele to store Kaworu series clones, so Kaworu's soul transfers there from above the Universe. There is also a multiverse, and a pinnacle of causality in it is obtained by joining a lance with a god-like being. Through the malleable causality, Shinji chooses the world without EVAs, in which he is able to develop as a normal human as if EVAs never existed. Any such changes in causality may spin a new loop in the original branch within the Universe with EVAs. Asuka chooses the part of the multiverse with the causality where EVAs exist, so Anno unifies endings from the original series and EoE.

[]: Re: Kaworu and the coffins             Quote Post by DantesInferno » Sun Aug 15, 2021 7:31 am Konja7 wrote: It's noticeable that the coffins really exist, since we can see these in 1.11. So, these coffins seem to have a real function, which seems to be the creation of Kaworu.

Article: Kaworu and the coffins
 Kurt Vonnegut. Kaworu may exist above the human Universe, and therefore above time, so death and life are the same for him, as he asserts at the original series. The coffins may just be a fancy way of Seele to store Kaworu series clones, so Kaworu's soul transfers there from above the Universe. There is also a multiverse, and a pinnacle of causality in it is obtained by joining a lance with a god-like being. Through the malleable causality, Shinji chooses the world without EVAs, in which he is able to develop as a normal human as if EVAs never existed. Any such changes in causality may spin a new loop in the original branch within the Universe with EVAs. Asuka chooses the part of the multiverse with the causality where EVAs exist, so Anno unifies endings from the original series and EoE.

[]: Re: Kaworu and the coffins             Quote Post by DantesInferno » Sun Aug 15, 2021 7:31 am Konja7 wrote: It's noticeable that the coffins really exist, since we can see these in 1.11. So, these coffins seem to have a real function, which seems to be the creation of Kaworu.
